raw_user_request: Build a Pomodoro timer for focus sessions.
concrete_goal: Create a browser-based Pomodoro timer for students to start, pause, reset, and toggle focus or break intervals from the first screen while tracking completed focus sessions.
success_criteria: Users can start, pause, reset, and toggle between focus and break modes without leaving the timer screen.
The timer shows remaining time, active mode, completed session count, empty first-run guidance, and saved settings state.
Desktop and mobile layouts keep timer controls readable without overlap or clipped labels.
scope_in: Single-page Pomodoro timer UI with local client-side state.
Focus, short break, long break, start, pause, reset, toggle, session count, validation, and responsive behavior.
scope_out: User accounts, calendar integrations, notifications, paid APIs, backend persistence, and production deployment.
assumptions: Local browser state is enough for the prototype.
Focus session lengths can use preset values without external configuration.
implementation_constraints: Do not add external services or paid APIs.
Keep the primary timer controls available on the first screen.

scenario_flows.name: Run focus timer session
preconditions: The Pomodoro timer is open with default focus and break durations.
steps: Reset the timer and toggle to break mode.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The timer updates remaining time, mode label, and completed session count while controls remain usable.
failure_or_edge_cases: Invalid duration input shows validation and disables save.
Small mobile viewport keeps start, pause, reset, and toggle controls reachable without overlap.

verification_matrix.test: Run available tests or add a focused timer behavior test if the project has a test harness.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Check start, pause, reset, toggle, validation, session count, desktop layout, mobile layout, and keyboard focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop and mobile screenshots plus behavior, accessibility, and state coverage for the timer controls.
iteration_policy: Rework until the review score reaches the quality threshold.
